task_completion.description: Verify that the CT Cranium DICOM scan is loaded and visible in the medical imaging software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a medical imaging application (e.g., InVesalius) displaying axial, sagittal, and coronal views of a skull CT scan.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent cuts the skull along the mid-sagittal plane.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent should be seen using a cropping, cutting, or masking tool to divide the 3D skull model or mask vertically down the middle (mid-sagittal plane).

task_completion.description: Verify that only the left half of the skull is retained as a 3D surface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer must display only one half of the skull, with the other half completely removed or hidden.

task_completion.description: Verify that the preserved half is the anatomical left side using orientation markers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The preserved half of the skull must correspond to the side marked 'L' (Left) or the anatomical left side according to the software's orientation markers.

task_completion.description: Verify that the resulting surface is exported as a binary STL file to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog or save prompt should be visible, showing the file being saved exactly as 'left_hemicranium.stl' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the software's GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should be seen clicking through the medical imaging software's menus and tools to cut and export the model, rather than using terminal commands or scripts to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: Ensure the 3D model was genuinely manipulated.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a progression from a full skull to a half skull in the 3D viewer, proving the agent actually performed the cut operation.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not just create a dummy file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent must not use a text editor or terminal command (like 'touch') to create an empty or fake 'left_hemicranium.stl' file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The required output file path is exactly '/home/ga/Documents/left_hemicranium.stl'. The valid left hemicranium has a width/length ratio of ~0.52 (roughly half the width of a full skull), a length of ~214.37mm, and a height of ~210.00mm. The VLM should verify that the 3D model visually matches a left hemicranium (cut down the middle) and is exported to the exact specified path.
